name: Ployes (Griddle Cakes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together the buckwheat flour, salt, and all-purpose flour in a bowl to form a thick paste.
2. Gradually add cold water, stirring until well combined.
3. Add boiling water and stir until smooth.
4. Stir in the baking powder and continue mixing until no lumps remain.
5. Heat a preheated medium-hot cast iron pan without butter.
6. Pour the batter into the hot pan, spreading it evenly.
7. Cook until small bubbles form on the surface of the ployes.
8. Flip the ployes carefully; they are cooked when the surface is covered with more bubbles.
9. Serve the ployes buttered or with molasses.
Traditional from the Madawaska region of New Brunswick.
